responses: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) is the process of analyzing and summarizing datasets to understand their structure, detect patterns, identify anomalies, and prepare data for modeling.
The main steps in EDA include understanding data, handling missing values, detecting outliers, analyzing distributions, checking correlations, feature engineering, and visualization.
A Pandas DataFrame is a two-dimensional labeled data structure with rows and columns, similar to a table in SQL or Excel.
A Pandas Series is a one-dimensional labeled array capable of holding any data type.

patterns: How to read csv in pandas?
responses: CSV files can be loaded using pd.read_csv(). It reads structured data into a Pandas DataFrame.
The head() function displays the first few rows of a dataset, while tail() shows the last few rows.
The info() function provides summary information including data types and non-null counts.
The describe() function generates statistical summary including mean, median, std, min and max.
NumPy arrays are multi-dimensional data structures optimized for numerical computations.

patterns: Difference between numpy array and list
responses: NumPy arrays are faster and more memory efficient compared to Python lists because they use contiguous memory allocation.
Missing values are data points that are not recorded or unavailable in a dataset.
Missing values can be detected using isnull() or isna() functions in Pandas.
Missing values can be removed using dropna() method.
Missing values can be filled using mean, median, mode or forward/backward filling.
An outlier is a data point significantly different from other observations.
Z-score measures how many standard deviations a value is from the mean.
IQR method detects outliers using Q1 and Q3 quartiles.
Feature scaling standardizes features to similar ranges to improve model performance.
Min-Max scaling transforms values into a fixed range between 0 and 1.
Standard scaling transforms data to mean 0 and standard deviation 1.
Label encoding converts categorical data into numeric labels.
One hot encoding creates binary columns for each category.
Correlation measures the relationship between two variables.
Pearson correlation measures linear relationship between variables.
Multicollinearity occurs when independent variables are highly correlated.
Covariance measures how two variables change together. A positive covariance indicates variables increase together, while negative covariance indicates inverse relationship.

patterns: Which is better correlation or covariance?
responses: Covariance shows direction of relationship, while correlation standardizes the measure between -1 and 1, making it easier to interpret.
Variance measures how far data points spread from the mean. It is the square of standard deviation.
Standard deviation measures dispersion of data around the mean.
Skewness measures asymmetry of distribution. Positive skew means long right tail, negative skew means long left tail.
Kurtosis measures tail heaviness of distribution. High kurtosis indicates more extreme outliers.
Log transformation reduces right skewness and stabilizes variance.
Box-Cox transformation makes data more normally distributed.
Power transformation adjusts skewed data to approximate normal distribution.
Feature selection is the process of selecting important variables to improve model performance.
Filter methods select features using statistical tests like correlation and chi-square.
Wrapper methods evaluate subsets of features using model performance.
Embedded methods perform feature selection during model training.
Variance threshold removes features with low variability.

patterns: How to detect multicollinearity using VIF?
responses: VIF measures how much variance of a regression coefficient increases due to multicollinearity.
Data leakage occurs when information from test data is used during training.
Train-test split divides data into training and testing sets to evaluate model performance.

patterns: Why use stratify in train test split?
responses: Stratified sampling preserves class distribution in training and testing data.
Data balancing techniques like SMOTE handle class imbalance in datasets.
SMOTE generates synthetic samples to balance minority classes.
Oversampling increases minority class samples.
Undersampling reduces majority class samples.
Distance-based algorithms like SVM and KNN are sensitive to feature scaling.
Data validation ensures dataset quality and consistency before modeling.
A data pipeline automates preprocessing steps like scaling, encoding, and transformation.
GroupBy in Pandas is used to split data into groups based on categorical variables and perform aggregation like sum, mean, count on each group.
Aggregation functions include sum(), mean(), median(), count(), min(), max(), and std(). They summarize grouped data.
A pivot table summarizes data by reshaping it into a matrix format using aggregation.
Merge combines two DataFrames based on a common column similar to SQL joins.
Concat is used to combine multiple DataFrames either vertically or horizontally.
The apply() function allows applying custom functions across rows or columns.
Lambda functions allow quick inline operations on DataFrame columns.
Duplicates can be detected using duplicated() and removed using drop_duplicates().
Boolean indexing filters data based on conditions.
Data can be sorted using sort_values() or sort_index().
Datetime columns can be converted using pd.to_datetime().
Time series EDA includes trend analysis, seasonality detection, and lag feature creation.
Lag features use previous time steps as predictors in time series modeling.
Rolling window computes statistics over sliding windows of data.
Expanding window calculates cumulative statistics from start to current index.
Feature interaction combines variables to capture non-linear relationships.
Binning converts continuous variables into categorical intervals.

patterns: Difference between one hot and label encoding
responses: One-hot encoding avoids ordinal relationships, while label encoding is suitable for ordered categories.
Scaling standardizes mean and variance, while normalization scales values to fixed range.
Data quality checks include completeness, consistency, uniqueness, and accuracy.
Missing data types include MCAR (completely random), MAR (random), and MNAR (not random).
A preprocessing pipeline automates scaling, encoding, and transformation steps.
Cross-validation splits data into multiple folds to validate model robustness.
Sampling selects a subset of data for analysis.
Feature importance indicates how much each variable contributes to prediction.
Data profiling is the process of analyzing datasets to understand structure, patterns, distributions, missing values, and anomalies before modeling.
Data auditing ensures data integrity, accuracy, completeness, and compliance with predefined standards.
Data consistency ensures that values follow logical rules and constraints across the dataset.

patterns: What is uniqueness in data quality?
responses: Uniqueness ensures there are no duplicate records in the dataset.
High cardinality refers to categorical variables with many unique values. Techniques include target encoding and frequency encoding.
Target encoding replaces categorical values with the mean of the target variable.
Frequency encoding replaces categories with their occurrence frequency.
Dimensionality reduction reduces the number of features while retaining important information.
PCA is a dimensionality reduction technique that transforms correlated variables into principal components.

patterns: Role of eigenvalues in dimensionality reduction
responses: Eigenvalues measure variance explained by components, while eigenvectors define direction of components.

patterns: How to choose number of components in PCA?
responses: Explained variance ratio shows how much variance each principal component captures.
Curse of dimensionality occurs when model performance decreases with increasing number of features.
Many statistical models assume data follows normal distribution.
Heteroscedasticity occurs when error variance changes across data.
Homoscedasticity means constant variance of errors across observations.
Standardization pipeline ensures consistent scaling during training and testing.
Industry strategies include automated validation, rule-based filtering, and anomaly detection.
Text EDA includes tokenization, word frequency analysis, and removing stopwords.
Image EDA includes checking dimensions, pixel distributions, and metadata analysis.
Frameworks like Great Expectations automate dataset validation rules.
Automated feature engineering generates features automatically using libraries like Featuretools.
Interaction terms capture combined effects of two or more features.
Rescaling ensures numeric features are comparable.
MNAR occurs when missingness depends on unobserved data.
Reproducibility ensures same results can be generated with same data and pipeline.
Trend analysis identifies long-term movement or direction in time series data.
Seasonality refers to repeating patterns occurring at fixed intervals in time series data.
Time series decomposition splits data into trend, seasonality, and residual components.
Autocorrelation measures correlation of a variable with its past values.
Partial autocorrelation measures correlation between observations excluding intermediate lags.
Data drift occurs when input data distribution changes over time.

patterns: Difference between data drift and concept drift
responses: Concept drift occurs when the relationship between input and output changes over time.
A feature store is a centralized repository to manage and reuse machine learning features.
Data versioning tracks changes in datasets to ensure reproducibility.
Memory optimization includes changing data types and using categorical encoding.
Categorical dtype reduces memory by storing categories efficiently.
Chunk processing reads large datasets in smaller parts to reduce memory load.
Data partitioning splits large datasets into manageable parts.
Sampling strategies include random sampling, stratified sampling, and systematic sampling.
Robust scaling uses median and IQR, making it resistant to outliers.
Rare categories can be grouped into an 'Other' category to reduce noise.
Schema validation ensures columns follow expected data types and formats.
Pipeline monitoring ensures data quality and detects failures in production systems.
Data reconciliation ensures consistency between datasets from different sources.
Missing value pattern analysis helps determine randomness of missing data.
Anomaly detection identifies unusual patterns that deviate from normal behavior.
Boxplot visualizes distribution, median, quartiles, and outliers.
Heatmap visualizes correlation matrix using color intensity.
Distribution analysis identifies skewness and spread of data.
Data scalability ensures systems can handle increasing data volumes.
Data governance defines policies to ensure data quality, security, and compliance.
Data security protects sensitive data using encryption and access control.
Standard error measures the accuracy of a sample mean by estimating variability between sample means.
A confidence interval estimates the range in which a population parameter is likely to fall.
Common normalization methods include Min-Max scaling, Z-score normalization, and Robust scaling.
Inconsistent data can be handled by standardizing formats, correcting typos, and validating categorical entries.
Validation rules define constraints such as allowed ranges, data types, and mandatory fields.
Duplicate entries can be detected and removed using drop_duplicates() method in pandas.
Missing values can be visualized using heatmaps or bar plots to detect patterns.
Outlier capping replaces extreme values with percentile thresholds.
Winsorization limits extreme values by replacing them with boundary percentiles.
Feature transformation modifies variables to improve model performance.
Data can be split into train, validation, and test sets to evaluate performance.
Holdout method splits dataset into training and testing sets once.
K-fold validation divides data into k subsets and rotates training/testing.
Stratified K-fold maintains class distribution in each fold.
Workflow includes cleaning, transformation, scaling, encoding, and splitting.
Automated cleaning uses scripts or frameworks to standardize preprocessing.
Big data EDA requires distributed processing tools like Spark.
Data compression reduces storage and speeds processing.
Indexing allows efficient row selection and labeling.
Hierarchical indexing allows multiple levels of indexing.
Sparse data contains many zero values and can be optimized using sparse matrices.
Data integrity ensures data remains accurate and consistent over time.
Standardization ensures consistent naming conventions and formats.
Time series missing values can be filled using interpolation.
Interpolation estimates missing values between known points.
Visual inspection helps detect anomalies and inconsistencies.
Summary statistics include mean, median, variance, and percentiles.
Quality metrics include completeness, accuracy, timeliness, and validity.
A model-ready dataset is cleaned, scaled, encoded, and split properly.
Industry EDA focuses on automation, reproducibility, scalability, and monitoring.
